questions.prompt: LoRA vs. Dreambooth — when use LoRA?

choices.text: For most custom training. Smaller files (10-200 MB vs. 7 GB), faster training (30 min - 4 hours vs. 8-24 hours), stackable (multiple LoRAs in one prompt), reusable across base models. 95% of fine-tuning is LoRA. Dreambooth reserved for: base models for ongoing series, max likeness, stylistic foundations, model merging.

questions.explanation: LoRA for most fine-tuning. Smaller, faster, stackable. Dreambooth for special cases (foundation models, max likeness).

questions.prompt: Trained character LoRA; output exactly matches training photos; can't pose in new scenes. Cause?

choices.text: Overfitting — LoRA memorized training images. Fix: fewer training steps, lower learning rate, more varied dataset, regularization images, lower Network Rank, earlier checkpoint. Iterate to dial settings. First training rarely perfect; expect 2-3 iterations.

questions.explanation: Overfitting. Fewer steps, lower learning rate, more varied dataset, regularization. Iterate.

questions.prompt: Counterintuitive captioning principle for character LoRA?

choices.text: What you DON'T caption gets baked in. If character always has blue hair: DON'T caption 'blue hair'; let it become part of trigger word's identity. Caption variable features (pose, clothing, background, lighting, expression); don't caption fixed signature features. Foundational principle; counterintuitive but essential.

questions.explanation: What you DON'T caption gets baked in. Caption variable; don't caption fixed signature features.

questions.prompt: Stacking 3 LoRAs at 1.0 each = burned output. Fix?

choices.text: Reduce weights. 3x 1.0 = 3x amplification. Try 0.6-0.8 each when stacking 2-3 LoRAs; 0.4-0.6 each for 4-5. Total combined influence should be 1-2. Test pairs systematically; some combinations incompatible regardless of weights.

questions.explanation: Reduce individual weights. 0.6-0.8 each for 2-3 stack. Some pairs incompatible.

questions.prompt: Maximum control over character + pose + composition?

choices.text: ControlNet + LoRA + IP-Adapter combined + text prompt. ControlNet for structure (pose / composition); LoRA for identity (trained character); IP-Adapter for style; text for scene. Pro-grade control stack; each layer addresses different axis. Standard for production / commercial / hero work.

questions.explanation: ControlNet + LoRA + IP-Adapter + text. Pro stack; each layer different axis of control.
